name: Crusaders' Square
region: Stratholme, just off the Market Row / Alonsus Chapel axis.

sensoryProfile.sight: An open square visible from the wall, broad enough to hold shields, horses, and reserve movement while nearby streets burn in separate slices.
sensoryProfile.sound: Runner traffic, barked orders, and the harder echo of organized movement through open stone instead of alley confinement.
sensoryProfile.smell: Smoke and hot stone carried out of the city center.
sensoryProfile.touch: Read at distance as a staging surface rather than intimate ground.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: A civic open space repurposed into one more clean lane inside massacre.

establishedFacts.fact: Crusaders' Square is a major open district of Stratholme tied to the city’s central civic / holy core.
In chapter 17 it serves as a visible staging lane and reserve knot during Arthas's purge.
Its position near Market Row helps make the Culling readable from the wall as organized district work instead of simple collapse.

eventsHere.event: Crusaders' Square functions as a holding and staging knot during the Culling, visible from the wall witness position.
